articles.title: Dame Shirley Bassey Stops Writing Back to Fans
articles.summary: Dame Shirley Bassey is a famous singer from Wales who is 89 years old. She has been singing for 70 years and has many fans around the world. She used to write back to fans who sent her letters, but now she cannot do that anymore. This is because she needs surgery on her hand, which makes it hard for her to write. She shared this news on Instagram and said she is sorry. She feels happy and honored that people send her letters about their lives and how her music helps them. Dame Shirley started singing when she was young and became a big star, singing songs for James Bond movies. She is a special person in Wales and has received awards for her singing.

articles.title: TV Show Says No to Old Joke Book
articles.summary: A TV show called The Repair Shop decided not to fix a joke book that belonged to Bob Monkhouse, a famous comedian who passed away. The book was brought to the show by his daughter and writing partner. The people making the show looked at the book and found many jokes that were not right for their program. They told the family and gave the book back without filming it. Bob Monkhouse was a well-known TV star for many years before he died in 2003. His joke books have a history, like being stolen once. The show usually fixes old items, including things from celebrities, but this time they chose not to because of the jokes.

articles.title: Jannik Sinner Breaks a Tennis Record
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner is a tennis player from Italy who has done something amazing. He won 26 sets in a row at big tennis tournaments called Masters 1000 events. This broke a record that Novak Djokovic held for 10 years. Sinner played against Corentin Moutet in Miami and won easily in two sets. He is now trying to win two tournaments in a row, which is called the 'Sunshine Double'. Sinner is happy and says tennis is unpredictable. Other players like Carlos Alcaraz and Daniil Medvedev are also mentioned, but Sinner is the star of this story.

articles.title: Tennis Stars Win Big Matches in Miami
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka, the top tennis player in the world, won her match quickly at the Miami Open tournament. She beat Zheng Qinwen in two sets, 6-3 and 6-4, in just over an hour. Sabalenka is a champion who has won the US Open twice. She will play Hailey Baptiste next. Coco Gauff, another tennis star, also won her match but it was harder. She played three sets against Sorana Cirstea and won 6-3, 4-6, 6-2. Gauff made some mistakes but still won. She is happy to play at her home tournament in Miami and wants to win, but she is not putting too much pressure on herself. Both players are moving to the next round called the quarter-finals.

articles.title: Iga Swiatek Changes Coaches After Tough Tennis Loss
articles.summary: Iga Swiatek is a famous tennis player who just stopped working with her coach, Wim Fissette. They started together in October 2024 and won big tournaments like Wimbledon, Seoul, and Cincinnati. But this year, Iga has not played as well, losing early in four tournaments, including the Miami Open where she lost to Magda Linette. This was her first early loss in 74 tournaments. Iga said she feels disappointed and has learned lessons. She decided to take a new path without her coach. She thanked him for helping her believe in herself and improve her game. Iga plays best when she feels clear and confident on the court.

articles.title: A New Leader for the BBC: Matt Brittin's Big Job
articles.summary: Matt Brittin is a former Google boss who might become the new leader of the BBC, a big TV and radio company in Britain. He left Google after 20 years and took a break, growing a beard and planning fun activities like scuba diving. Now, he could get a very important job where many people will watch his every move. The BBC has had some problems lately, like mistakes and people leaving, so workers want a strong leader. People who know Brittin say he is smart, good at solving problems, and makes others feel happy and listened to. They think he can help the BBC make tough decisions and improve. But this job is hard because everyone, including politicians and employees, will criticize him. Brittin's experience at Google, where he cut jobs, might help him make changes at the BBC.
